expectations.text: States that get_restricted_merge_ids is SpikeSortingOutput-only (not on the _Merge base)
expectations.evidence: Says 'It is defined only on SpikeSortingOutput, not on the shared _Merge base class'.

expectations.text: Names the correct replacement methods for PositionOutput: merge_restrict and/or merge_get_part
Explains that calling it on the wrong master raises AttributeError (not silent wrong data)
expectations.evidence: States 'Python raises AttributeError at attribute-lookup time. That's good news for you: it fails loudly instead of silently returning the wrong thing.'

expectations.text: Does not recommend subclassing or monkeypatching PositionOutput
expectations.evidence: Says 'Don't try to fix this by subclassing or monkey-patching PositionOutput'.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend calling `get_restricted_merge_ids` on `PositionOutput` (or any non-`SpikeSortingOutput` master). Quoting the user's failed call to explain why it raises is fine; recommending it as the path forward is not.
expectations.evidence: Final TL;DR concludes 'not PositionOutput().get_restricted_merge_ids(...), which will AttributeError'.
